prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='rf', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.9373) and high seasonality variance (1.0867), with early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS, ARIMA) differing significantly from late horizon specialists (ONLY_FT_rf, ONLY_FT_svr, NaiveMovingAverage). The trend champion is rf (lowest slope error 0.5428) while THETA dominates seasonality capture (0.6627 correlation). Given the high disagreement and seasonal variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths rather than simple averaging.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "THETA",
  "overall_champion": "NaiveMovingAverage",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "ONLY_FT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_FT_svr", "ONLY_FT_rf", "ARIMA", "ETS", "THETA", "CWT_svr", "rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_CWT_catboost", "catboost", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "NaiveSeasonal", "FT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "FT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.9373,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.9373) and high seasonality variance (1.0867), with early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS, ARIMA) differing significantly from late horizon specialists (ONLY_FT_rf, ONLY_FT_svr, NaiveMovingAverage). The trend champion is rf (lowest slope error 0.5428) while THETA dominates seasonality capture (0.6627 correlation). Given the high disagreement and seasonal variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths rather than simple averaging."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 weighted (exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), 2 selection (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3, best_per_horizon_by_validation). No leakage issues - all use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection with n_windows=3. Candidate set is not homogeneous (≥2 different types present). No redundancy requiring pruning. Universe leaderboards show some candidates like topk_mean_per_horizon_k6 and poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim1.0 perform well but current set already has diverse representation. When_good: candidate set is balanced with baseline, weighted, and selection types; no changes needed.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": "candidate_set_balanced"
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.9373) and high seasonality variance (1.0867), with early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS, ARIMA) differing significantly from late horizon specialists (ONLY_FT_rf, ONLY_FT_svr, NaiveMovingAverage). The trend champion is rf (lowest slope error 0.5428) while THETA dominates seasonality capture (0.6627 correlation). Given the high disagreement and seasonal variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage diverse model strengths rather than simple averaging.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread ratio=0.9373 shows extreme model dispersion (best NaiveMovingAverage at 8814 vs worst DWT_svr at 20830). Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high disagreement and seasonal variance (seasonality_corr_variance=1.0867). With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.35 prevents overfitting. Exp-weighted averaging adds diversity to capture trend champions (rf) and seasonality champions (THETA).

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 weighted (exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35), 2 selection (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3, best_per_horizon_by_validation). No leakage issues - all use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection with n_windows=3. Candidate set is not homogeneous (≥2 different types present). No redundancy requiring pruning. Universe leaderboards show some candidates like topk_mean_per_horizon_k6 and poly_weighted_average_p0.5_trim1.0 perform well but current set already has diverse representation. When_good: candidate set is balanced with baseline, weighted, and selection types; no changes needed.
